description_long: This water point is a tall, ornate structure with a square stone base supporting a decorative column. The base features carved panels and a semicircular basin on one side, likely for water collection or dispensing. The column is segmented, with a bulbous finial at the top, and the entire structure appears to be made of stone or concrete with some weathering and moss at the base. The front side has a visible spout above the basin, while the other sides are adorned with decorative reliefs and arches. The structure is set on a slightly raised platform, suggesting it is intended for public or communal use. The overall design is reminiscent of historical or Victorian-era public fountains commonly found in parks or urban squares.
description: A tall, decorative stone water point with a column, ornate base, and a semicircular basin for water collection. Features carved panels and a prominent spout on the front.
description_view_0: Front view showing the semicircular basin, spout, and detailed carvings on the base.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the arched decorative panel and the side profile of the basin.
description_view_2: Rear view with a plain arched panel and no visible spout or basin.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with another arched panel and partial view of the basin's side.
attribute_spout_type: short, straight metal spout projecting horizontally above the basin
attribute_surrounding_infrastructure: concrete platform with slight elevation and moss at the base
